title: ଦଧିବାମନ ଜୀଉଙ୍କ ଚତୁର୍ଦ୍ଧା ମୂର୍ତ୍ତିଙ୍କ ଦେବସ୍ନାନ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ନୀତି ସମ୍ପନ୍ନ
description:
ପାଟଣା, ୨୯।୬ (ବୀର କିଶୋର ଦାଶ): କେନ୍ଦୁଝର ଜିଲା ପାଟଣା ବ୍ଲକ ଅନ୍ତର୍ଗତ ରାଜନଗରସ୍ଥିତ ପ୍ରାଚୀନ ଶ୍ରୀଶ୍ରୀ ଦଧିବାମନ ଜୀଉ ମନ୍ଦିରରେ ସୋମବାର ପବିତ୍ର ଦେବସ୍ନାନ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ମହୋତ୍ସବ ଭକ୍ତିଭାବ ଓ ପାରମ୍ପରିକ ରୀତିନୀତି ଅନୁଯାୟୀ ଅନୁଷ୍ଠିତ ହୋଇଯାଇଛି। ମନ୍ଦିର ପରିସରସ୍ଥ ସ୍ନାନବେଦୀରେ ଚତୁର୍ଦ୍ଧା ମୂର୍ତ୍ତିଙ୍କ ଦିବ୍ୟ ସ୍ନାନ ନୀତି ସମ୍ପନ୍ନ ହୋଇଥିଲା। ଏହି ଅବସରରେ ମନ୍ଦିର ପରିସରରେ ଥିବା ପବିତ୍ର କୂଅରୁ ସଂଗୃହୀତ ୧୦୮ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣକୁମ୍ଭ ଜଳରେ ମହାପ୍ରଭୁଙ୍କ ମହାସ୍ନାନ ନୀତି ସମ୍ପନ୍ନ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ସମସ୍ତ […]
The post ଦଧିବାମନ ଜୀଉଙ୍କ ଚତୁର୍ଦ୍ଧା ମୂର୍ତ୍ତିଙ୍କ ଦେବସ୍ନାନ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ନୀତି ସମ୍ପନ୍ନ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.
content:
ପାଟଣା, ୨୯।୬ (ବୀର କିଶୋର ଦାଶ): କେନ୍ଦୁଝର ଜିଲା ପାଟଣା ବ୍ଲକ ଅନ୍ତର୍ଗତ ରାଜନଗରସ୍ଥିତ ପ୍ରାଚୀନ ଶ୍ରୀଶ୍ରୀ ଦଧିବାମନ ଜୀଉ ମନ୍ଦିରରେ ସୋମବାର ପବିତ୍ର ଦେବସ୍ନାନ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ମହୋତ୍ସବ ଭକ୍ତିଭାବ ଓ ପାରମ୍ପରିକ ରୀତିନୀତି ଅନୁଯାୟୀ ଅନୁଷ୍ଠିତ ହୋଇଯାଇଛି। ମନ୍ଦିର ପରିସରସ୍ଥ ସ୍ନାନବେଦୀରେ ଚତୁର୍ଦ୍ଧା ମୂର୍ତ୍ତିଙ୍କ ଦିବ୍ୟ ସ୍ନାନ ନୀତି ସମ୍ପନ୍ନ ହୋଇଥିଲା।
ଏହି ଅବସରରେ ମନ୍ଦିର ପରିସରରେ ଥିବା ପବିତ୍ର କୂଅରୁ ସଂଗୃହୀତ ୧୦୮ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣକୁମ୍ଭ ଜଳରେ ମହାପ୍ରଭୁଙ୍କ ମହାସ୍ନାନ ନୀତି ସମ୍ପନ୍ନ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ସମସ୍ତ ନୀତିକାନ୍ତି ଶାସ୍ତ୍ରବିଧି ଅନୁଯାୟୀ ମନ୍ଦିରର ସେବାୟତମାନେ ନିଷ୍ଠାର ସହ ସମ୍ପାଦନ କରିଥିଲେ।
ଦେବସ୍ନାନ ନୀତିରେ ବ୍ରହ୍ମା ଭାବେ ପ୍ରଦୀପ କୁମାର ମିଶ୍ର, ପରିଛା ଦୁର୍ଯ୍ୟୋଧନ ମିଶ୍ର, ଠାକୁରଙ୍କ ପହଣ୍ଡିଆ ବ୍ରାହ୍ମଣ ସତ୍ୟନାରାୟଣ ମିଶ୍ର, ମନରଞ୍ଜନ ମିଶ୍ର, ଅକ୍ଷୟ ନାରାୟଣ ମିଶ୍ର, ମୃତ୍ୟୁଞ୍ଜୟ ପାଣି, ଘନଶ୍ୟାମ ମିଶ୍ର, ଜଗନ୍ନାଥ ପଣ୍ଡା, ଶଶୀରଞ୍ଜନ ମିଶ୍ର, ସେବାୟତ ଗୋପୀନାଥ ପଣ୍ଡା, ମାଳି ସେବକ ପର୍ଶୁରାମ ରଣା, ତନ୍ତୀ ସେବକ ମଦନମୋହନ ବେହେରା, ରଜକ ସେବକ ପ୍ରଫୁଲ୍ଲ ସେଠୀ, ଛତ୍ରଧାରୀ ହେମରଞ୍ଜନ ନାୟକ ଓ ଅର୍ଜୁନଚରଣ ବେହେରା ପ୍ରମୁଖ ବିଭିନ୍ନ ସେବାରେ ନିୟୋଜିତ ଥିଲେ। ଏହାସହ ୨୨ ନିଯୋଗର ସେବାୟତମାନେ ମଧ୍ୟ ଠାକୁରଙ୍କ ସମସ୍ତ ନୀତିକାନ୍ତିରେ ସହଯୋଗ କରିଥିଲେ।
ପବିତ୍ର ଦେବସ୍ନାନ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ଅବସରରେ ସ୍ନାନମଣ୍ଡପରେ ବିରାଜମାନ ଚତୁର୍ଦ୍ଧା ମୂର୍ତ୍ତିଙ୍କ ଦର୍ଶନ ପାଇଁ ହଜାର ହଜାର ଶ୍ରଦ୍ଧାଳୁଙ୍କ ସମାଗମ ହୋଇଥିଲା। ଭକ୍ତମାନେ ମହାପ୍ରଭୁଙ୍କ ଦିବ୍ୟ ସ୍ନାନ ଦର୍ଶନ କରି ଆଶୀର୍ବାଦ ଲାଭ କରିବା ସହ ଧନ୍ୟ ଅନୁଭବ କରିଥିଲେ।
କୀର୍ତ୍ତନ, ଘଣ୍ଟ, ଶଙ୍ଖଧ୍ୱନି, ହୁଳହୁଳି ଓ ବିଭିନ୍ନ ବାଦ୍ୟର ଧ୍ୱନିରେ ସମଗ୍ର ମନ୍ଦିର ପରିସର ଭକ୍ତିମୟ ପରିବେଶରେ ଉଛୁଳି ଉଠିଥିଲା। ଶେଷରେ ଉପସ୍ଥିତ ଶ୍ରଦ୍ଧାଳୁମାନଙ୍କୁ ଶୀତଳ ଭୋଗ ବଣ୍ଟନ କରାଯାଇଥିଲା। ପବିତ୍ର ଦେବସ୍ନାନ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ଉତ୍ସବକୁ ନେଇ ସମଗ୍ର ରାଜନଗର ଅଞ୍ଚଳ ଭକ୍ତି ଓ ଉତ୍ସବର ପରିବେଶରେ ମୁଖରିତ ହୋଇଉଠିଥିଲା।

Dharitri – Odisha’s No.1 Odia Daily

The post ଦଧିବାମନ ଜୀଉଙ୍କ ଚତୁର୍ଦ୍ଧା ମୂର୍ତ୍ତିଙ୍କ ଦେବସ୍ନାନ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ନୀତି ସମ୍ପନ୍ନ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.

title: The future is electric. But when that future is… that is difficult to predict
description: In this era of technological upheavals, the signals emanating from the global automotive industry could leave you confused. When it comes to choosing which to focus on—internal combustion, electric vehicles, hybrids or plug-in hybrids—it is pretty much different strokes for different folks. In the midst of this, Mercedes-Benz is keeping its mind, and options, wide open. Mathias Geisen, who joined the company’s global board of management in February 2025 and looks after sales and customer experience, explains to Forbes India why the German luxury vehicles giant is letting the customer choose. Excerpts from an interview edited for clarity and brevity:Q. As the global head of sales and customer experience, how do you see the electric segment of the luxury car market evolving in India?India is a very interesting market. If you look at the so-called top-end segment, we have expanded globally from 11 percent in 2019 to 15 percent last year. That is a pretty strong number and important for us because we want to keep those customers with our brand for long. In this top-end segment, we invest a lot to have new products, new craftsmanship options, to make sure those customers appreciate what we offer them.If you now look at India, that 15 percent is 25 percent—a much higher share of top-end sales. So, it is not just that we have been market leaders for the last 11 years in a row in India, it is also that in this top-end segment, we are very, very well-positioned.In the luxury segment, in general, worldwide, which is pretty comparable to India, on average roughly 10 percent is electric. This is heavily increasing because of the new products we have launched in Europe—they will come to other regions as well, but we first launched them in Europe. We are sitting at twice that number and going in the right direction.In India, it is interesting to see that electric is 20 percent in the top-end segment. So, in the top-end segment there is strong interest in battery electric vehicles.Q. Where do you see the new buyers coming from? Are your buyers getting younger? Is their thinking different?Talking about the customer… big difference. In India, the customers are much younger. The average customer in India is at the end of his 30s. Rest of the world, excluding China, it is around, plus or minus 50. The customers come from another generation, which has grown up on technology and is willing to invest in something substantial. Which is why we see this premiumisation. They want more equipment in the vehicle. They appreciate new technology and the craftsmanship in their cars. This is the sophistication we see specially in India. So, when we launch a new product for this market, we look specifically at the specifications for markets where customer demand is slightly different from the global requirement.Also Read: JLR bets on hybrids and US as it looks to get back on growth pathQ. What would be the average age in China of a Mercedes buyer?Also below 40. Globally, if you look at the automotive landscape, nobody knows for sure which way things are going. For instance, Ferrari launched the Luce.I am happy that two weeks earlier we launched our AMG GT electric and received a lot of feedback. It is really…. you got to drive it. Everyone who has been in this vehicle… with its performance… more than 1,100 horsepower (1,169). This is really amazing. [It’s a] very new segment, these high-end electric sports cars.Q. Another high-end player, Lamborghini, does not want to do electric. What was meant to be its first electric car will now come as a plug-in hybrid. You are also big on plug-in hybrids. How do you see the mix of powertrains evolving in India?I think coming up with a forecast when it comes to electric mobility is difficult. I was head of corporate strategy five years ago and I definitely overestimated the speed with which customers will be willing to switch to electric mobility. On the other hand, now that we have new products launched in Europe, it is incredible how the pickup rates are. More than 40 percent of the order intake in Europe now is fully electric. I’m not speaking of plug-in hybrids but fully electric. It shows for the right products, with great design and great technology and great everyday usability… now there is a strong pull in the market for those products.It is still too difficult to say how they will develop market to market. That is why we have this strategic flexibility in our powertrain offerings. There will be markets where combustion engines will play a role in the 2030s. So, we cannot stop offering something. We have to build what our customers want. That means we have high complexity now. We offer petrol engines, diesel engines, all electrified, we offer plug-in hybrids and battery electric vehicles as well. But we have to do that because market requirements are so, so different. Even if the markets are right next to each other. Norway, Sweden and Denmark, for example, are completely different, heavily affected by regulations as well.So, we make sure the customer can get the same iconic design of the car and can choose what powertrain they want. We also believe for India plug-in hybrids can be an interesting alternative, which helps especially in those markets where the infrastructure may not be where it could already be. That is why plug-in hybrids were very strong in Europe. But now, with the increase of infrastructure in Europe, and, in parallel, the new offerings, battery electric vehicles have started to outperform plug-in hybrids.We are not teachers, we don’t want to educate the customers. The customer has to decide what he wants to have. We will make sure we have attractive offerings for him. The electric Mercedes-AMG GT 4-door coupé was officially revealed in late May. Photo courtesy Mercedes-BenzQ. But your target of going all electric by 2039 remains in place.Absolutely. Ambition 2039 remains in place. We also believe the future is electric. But when that future is… that is difficult to predict.Also Read: Can Mahindra do an SUV in EVs?Q. Any great marketing success works because you tell a story. What is the story electric vehicles in your market segment have to tell?At the beginning of electrification, years back, the customers wanted to show to the outside world they drove something different, that they were very much into new technology. They were the early adopters. That is why our early electric cars looked different from the combustion engine cars.Drastically different. That was the right assumption in those days, but that has changed. Over the years the customer said, ‘You know what, we love the technology. It is quiet, there is immediate torque, it is attractive from total cost of ownership, etc. But why does it have to look different from your beautiful combustion engine vehicles?’This is why we are, step by step, closing the zipper because customer requirements have changed. The early adopters were more into expressing, ‘I want to have something different’. Now they have kind of balanced out and we are more in a phase where, pretty much globally, customers say, ‘Hey, just make the vehicles look great, but I will tell you what powertrain I want to have’.Q. You have followed a philosophy of value over volume for at least four years. How is that panning out when your primary competitor is going hard at volumes with an entry point lower than yours?We call it profitable growth. Of course, we want to grow, but in a profitable and sustainable way. Our customers—this is pretty much a global pattern—say they buy a Mercedes-Benz because it has reasonable residual value and that is important for them, that they buy something that is still worth something after a while.Our intention is not just to push volumes for the sake of having big numbers and market share. We want to do that with a balanced approach to profitably and sustainably grow the business.Q. When you say profitable growth, is the emphasis on growth or profits?(Laughs) Within the company, people have been asking that question for 30 years. I have been with the company since 1998. And since 1998 we have said we want to grow profitably. And every employee asks, ‘What do you want now? Do you want profit or do you want to grow?’We indeed want to do both, and that works. You can grow without doing everything just for the sake of growing. It also means you need strategic patience. I cannot come to India and say it is a sprint. It is a marathon. You got to get rolling, you got to continuously invest, you have ups and downs, but you will finally win the marathon if you do it with a long-term perspective. It will be wrong to just push out volumes to risk residual values, risk lease rates, just to say, ‘Hey, I sold some thousand units more than you’. You have to do that in a reasonable and balanced way. We have to create a pull in the market with the right products and I’d say we are on a good track with that.

title: Alia Bhatt says daughter Raha is ‘meant for the stage’; how to spot talent early
অহা বছৰ বিতৰণৰ বাবে লিচুৰ অতি কমেও ২ লাখ পুলি প্ৰস্তুত কৰাৰ আহ্বান মন্ত্ৰী পীযুষৰ
description:
ডিজিটেল সংবাদ, তেজপুৰ : বিশ্বজুৰি জনপ্ৰিয়তা অৰ্জন কৰা তেজপুৰ বিখ্যাত লিচুৰ উৎপাদনৰ বিভিন্ন দিশৰ বুজ লোৱাৰ উদ্দেশ্যে কৃষি মন্ত্ৰী পীযুষ হাজৰীকাই সোমবাৰে তেজপুৰৰ পৰমাইঘূলীস্থিত লিচু বাগান পৰিদৰ্শন কৰে আৰু স্থানীয় লিচু খেতিয়কসকলৰ সৈতে এক মতবিনিময় কাৰ্যসূচীত মিলিত হয়। ভ্ৰমণকালত মন্ত্ৰীগৰাকীয়ে খেতিয়ক তথা কৃষক উৎপাদক কোম্পানীসমূহৰ (FPC) বিভিন্ন সমস্যাৰ বুজ লয়।

তেওঁ উল্লেখ কৰে যে লিচুৰ উৎপাদন আৰু ৰপ্তানি বৃদ্ধি কৰাৰ লক্ষ্যৰে কৃষি বিভাগে অতি শীঘ্ৰেই লিচু ৰপ্তানিকাৰক আৰু কৃষকসকলৰ এক বৈঠকৰ আয়েজন কৰিব। কৃষকসকলৰ সৈতে হোৱা বাৰ্তালাপত মন্ত্ৰীগৰাকীয়ে পোক-পৰুৱাৰ উপদ্ৰৱ, এফ.পি.চি.সমূহৰ বাবে ৰাজসাহায্যযুক্ত ট্ৰেক্টৰ আৰু পাৱাৰটিলাৰৰ প্ৰয়োজনীয়তা, সৌৰশক্তি চালিত লাইট আদি গুৰুত্বপূৰ্ণ বিষয়সমূহৰ বুজ লয়।
মন্ত্ৰী হাজৰীকাই ৰাজ্যৰ ভিন্ন প্ৰান্তৰ কৃষকৰ মাজত বিতৰণ কৰিব পৰাকৈ তেজপুৰৰ লিচু খেতিয়কসকলক অতি কমেও ২ লাখ লিচুৰ পুলি প্ৰস্তুত কৰিবলৈ আহ্বান জনায়। কৃষকৰ আয় বৃদ্ধি কৰাৰ বাবে লিচু, নাৰিকল, তামোল আদিৰ দৰে লাভজনক নগদ শস্যৰ উৎপাদন বৃদ্ধিৰ ওপৰত তেওঁ বিশেষভাৱে গুৰুত্ব আৰোপ কৰে। তেওঁ কয় যে অহা বছৰৰ পৰা প্ৰতিটো সমষ্টিত এনেধৰণৰ ১ লাখকৈ পুলি বিতৰণৰ লক্ষ্য স্থিৰ কৰিছে কৃষি বিভাগে।

মন্ত্ৰীগৰাকীয়ে লগতে আহ্বান জনাই কয় যে অসমৰ যুৱ প্ৰজন্মই এনেধৰণৰ লাভজনক খেতিৰ সৈতে জড়িত হোৱা উচিত। ম’বাইল টাৱাৰৰ বিকিৰণ আদিৰ দৰে অপ্ৰমাণিত কাৰণসমূহৰ অচিলা লৈ নাৰিকল, তামোল আদি ৰোপণৰ পৰা বিৰত থকাৰ পৰিৱৰ্তে নিজৰ ভূমিসমূহ সঠিকভাৱে ব্যৱহাৰ কৰিবলৈ তেওঁ যুৱ প্ৰজন্মক আহ্বান জনায়। আজিৰ অনুষ্ঠানত মন্ত্ৰীগৰাকীয়ে কৃষকসকলৰ মাজত স্প্ৰেয়িং মেচিন আৰু অন্যান্য কৃষি সঁজুলিও বিতৰণ কৰে।

উক্ত কাৰ্যসূচীত তেজপুৰৰ বিধায়ক পৃথ্বীৰাজ ৰাভা, কৃষি বিভাগৰ সচিব তথা সঞ্চালক উদয় প্ৰবীণ, শোণিতপুৰৰ জিলা উপায়ুক্ত, উদ্যান কৃষি বিভাগৰ সঞ্চালক আৰু অন্যান্য জ্যেষ্ঠ বিষয়াসকল উপস্থিত থাকে।

title: Donald Trump and how strongman leaders fall
description: The downfalls of would-be autocrats in Hungary, Brazil and the Philippines hold lessons for America

title: Star Couples Neha Dhupia-Angad Bedi & Sonakshi Sinha-Zaheer Iqbal Step Out In Style | WATCH
description: Bollywood star couples Neha Dhupia-Angad Bedi and Sonakshi Sinha-Zaheer Iqbal were spotted together in Mumbai's Bandra suburb while shooting a fun 'double date' concept project. The foursome shared laughs, playful banter, and great chemistry as they hit the local padel courts, serving major friendship and fitness goals. Dressed in stylish athleisure looks, the celebrity couples looked effortlessly chic while interacting with each other and the paparazzi. title: Europe Is Burning—7 Heatwave Survival Lessons It Can Borrow From India
description: As Europe battles record-breaking heat, India's time-tested summer habits offer practical lessons, from smart hydration and lighter meals to avoiding peak sun, that can help people stay safer during extreme heatwaves.
content: Europe is enduring one of its most intense and dangerous heatwaves in recent history. Temperatures have soared above 40°C across several countries, emergency rooms have reported surges in heat-related illnesses, wildfires are spreading, and the extreme heat has even warped roads and damaged transport infrastructure in parts of France and Germany. In Paris, people have queued for fans as demand skyrocketed, while parks, rivers and public fountains have become crowded refuges from the scorching weather. France has also reported 1,000+ deaths in the last 3 days just due to heat. Read More: 'I'm Too Old for Surgery' Is a Myth: Why More Indian Seniors Are Replacing Their KneesFor millions of Indians, however, living through 40°C-plus summers is nothing new. While India's heatwaves remain a serious public health challenge, decades of experience have also shaped practical habits that help people reduce their risk. As climate change makes extreme heat more common worldwide, these everyday lessons are becoming increasingly relevant.1. Respect the afternoon sunIn many parts of India, people instinctively avoid unnecessary outdoor activity between noon and 4 pm during peak summer. Markets slow down, outdoor work is rescheduled where possible, and people plan errands for early mornings or evenings. Rather than trying to ''push through' the heat, adjusting daily routines can significantly reduce the risk of heat exhaustion.2. Hydration is a habit—not an emergencyHydration should always be treated as a habit and in a heatwave, it can quickly escalate to an emergency situation. Thus, one should not wait till the very last moment to drink water. In India, staying hydrated is often one of the major constants throughout the routine of the day. In India, we also have a culture of relying on other hydrating beverages like aam panna, ORS, buttermilk, coconut water and nimbu pani throughout the day which do a good job in comparison to coffee and wine which may seem okay but are dehydrating in nature. The beverages prepared here help replace both fluids and electrolytes lost through sweating. During heatwaves, sipping water regularly is far more effective than drinking large amounts all at once.3. Dress for survival, not fashionLoose cotton clothing is the outfit of the summer. The fabric should be breathable and the colours should be light. This has always been India's summer uniform. These materials allow the sweat to evaporate efficiently and this in turn cools down the body naturally without requiring AC. Tight synthetic or polyester clothing, by contrast traps heat and moisture inside. 4. Make your home work with the weatherLong before widespread air conditioning, Indian homes relied on practical cooling techniques. Keeping the curtains closed during the hottest hours of the day, opening the windows only after sunset, using proper cross-ventilation and cooling rooms before bedtime can help in lowering indoor temperatures considerably. Many in India also use Khus curtains to keep the house cool. These simple measures also reduce the one's dependency on ACs during power-intensive heatwaves. 5. Eat lighter mealsHeavy, rich meals increase the body's metabolic heat production. During the summer months, many Indian households naturally shift towards foods that are lighter on the stomach, like, curd, salads, simple home-cooked meals with lentils and all. There are also seasonal fruits with high water content like watermelon, muskmelon and more that are popular choices because they help in maintaining hydration while providing all the essential elements and nutrients. 6. Watch out for the vulnerableOne of India's strongest community and cultural habits is regularly checking on the elderly relatives, looking up to the neighbours and young children during times of extreme weather conditions and that includes heatwaves. Older adults, infants and people with chronic illnesses are the groups especially vulnerable to heat-related illnesses. A quick phone call, offering drinking water or ensuring someone has access to a cool space can sometimes prevent a medical emergency.7. Treat heat as a health risk—not just uncomfortable weatherPerhaps the biggest lesson is psychological. In many parts of India, a heatwave isn't simply considered 'good weather.' It is treated as a genuine health hazard. People recognise warning signs such as dizziness, confusion, headache, nausea, excessive fatigue or unusually high body temperature as reasons to seek shade, hydrate immediately and, if symptoms worsen, obtain medical care. This awareness saves lives.A Changing Climate Requires Changing HabitsEurope's recent heatwave has shown how quickly extreme temperatures can overwhelm infrastructure and public health systems that were never designed for prolonged periods above 40°C. Scientists warn that such events are becoming more frequent as the climate warms, making adaptation just as important as mitigation.India certainly hasn't solved the challenge of extreme heat, and heatwaves continue to claim lives every year. But generations of experience have also produced practical, low-cost habits that anyone can adopt. As summers grow hotter across the globe, these simple practices may prove just as valuable in Paris and London as they already are in Delhi, Jaipur or Ahmedabad.Read More: India’s Missing Monsoon Is Taking a Toll on Your Body - Doctors Explain the Silent Heat Stress

title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 Tipped to Support Fast Charging, Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Might Arrive With a Larger Battery
description: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 (pictured) features a dual rear camera setup
content: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 will be launched with support for faster wired charging than its predecessor, a tipster claims. The clamshell-style foldable is said to launch with support for 45W wired fast charging. For reference, the Galaxy Z Flip 7, which was launched in July 2025, supports 25W (wired) and 15W (wireless) fast charging. The handset is equipped with a 4,300mAh battery. On the other hand, the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra and the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 might arrive with 5,000mAh and 4,800mAh batteries, respectively, while offering the same charging support as the Galaxy Z Flip 8.

title: Bharat Petroleum to buy 40% stake in Tiki Tar and Shell India for Rs 85 crore
description: The acquisition, which has received approval from the Department of Investment and Public Asset Management (DIPAM), is expected to be completed within 90 days
content: State-run Bharat Petroleum Corporation on Monday said it will acquire a 40 per cent equity stake in Tiki Tar and Shell India for Rs 85 crore in cash, as it seeks to expand its presence in India's fast-growing value-added bitumen market.The acquisition, which has received approval from the Department of Investment and Public Asset Management (DIPAM), is expected to be completed within 90 days, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd (BPCL) said in a regulatory filing.The transaction is not a related-party deal.Incorporated in October 2019, Tiki Tar and Shell India Pvt Ltd (TTSIPL) manufactures and markets bitumen and bituminous products used in highways and airport runways.Its portfolio includes VG Grade Bitumen, Polymer Modified Bitumen (PMB), Crumb Rubber Modified Bitumen (CRMB), and emulsions.The company also exports to Nepal, Bhutan and Bangladesh.BPCL said the investment aligns with its strategy to tap growing demand for value-added bitumen driven by India's infrastructure expansion.TTSIPL reported a revenue of Rs 404.6 crore in FY26 compared to Rs 545.2 crore in FY25 and Rs 317.8 crore in FY24. The company has an authorised share capital of Rs 37 crore and a paid-up capital of about Rs 36.1 crore.BPCL Share PriceShares of Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd closed at Rs 301.35, down 2.74 per cent on the BSE on Monday. The stock has touched a 52-week high of Rs 391.85 and a 52-week low of Rs 266.55 on the BSE.In the last five years, the PSU stock has gained 30 per cent from Rs 230 to Rs 300.BPCL Q4 FY26 PerformanceIn the fourth quarter of FY26, BPCL reported a net profit of Rs 3,191 crore.Revenue remained broadly stable at Rs 1.18 lakh crore, while EBITDA stood at Rs 10,061 crore.According to BPCL, petrol sales in Delhi crossed 27,800 metric tonnes (MT) in May 2026, compared with over 27,100 MT in the corresponding month last year, registering a growth of around 2.5 per cent.

title: যুদ্ধত নামিব ৰ’বট...
description:
বৰ্তমানৰ যুগত মানৱ জাতিয়ে তথ্য-প্ৰযুক্তিৰ দিশত বহু আগবাঢ়িছে। কৃত্ৰিম বুদ্ধিমত্তাৰ পৰা আৰম্ভ কৰি বিভিন্ন অত্যাধুনিক যন্ত্ৰলৈকে সকলোতে মানুহে সফলতা লাভ কৰিছে। আৰু এইবাৰ যুদ্ধক্ষেত্ৰত ৰ’বটক ব্যৱহাৰ কৰাৰ দিশত আগবাঢ়িছে মানৱ জাতি। এই সন্দৰ্ভত অসমীয়া প্ৰতিদিন ডিজিটেলৰ ৰাজৰ্ষি চহৰীয়াৰ এক প্ৰতিবেদন।

হলিউড বা বলিউডৰ এনে বহু চলচ্চিত্ৰ আছে য’ত ৰ’বট (Robot)ক সেনা বাহিনীৰ জোৱান বা দেশৰ নিৰাপত্তাৰ বাবে ব্যৱহাৰ কৰা দেখা যায়। সেয়া সম্পূৰ্ণৰূপে কাল্পনিক কাহিনী। কিন্তু এতিয়া সেই কল্পনাকেই যেন বাস্তৱ ৰূপ দিয়া হৈছে। 
বিশ্বৰ বহু কেইখন দেশৰ মাজত যুদ্ধ আৰম্ভ হোৱা পৰিলক্ষিত হৈছে বিগত কেইটামান মাহত। যুদ্ধত বহু কেইখন দেশে নিজৰ নাগৰিকৰ লগতে হেৰুৱাবলগীয়া হৈছে সেনা বাহিনীৰ জোৱানকো। নিজ নিজ দেশৰ নিৰাপত্তাৰ বাবে প্ৰাণ আহুতি দি অহা বীৰ জোৱানসকলৰ পৰিৱৰ্তে যদি ফ্ৰন্ট লাইনত ৰ’বটৰ ব্যৱহাৰ কৰা হয় তেন্তে জোৱানসকলৰ জীৱন ৰক্ষা পৰিব।
উল্লেখ্য যে, কেইমাহ মান পূৰ্বে ৰাছিয়া-ইউক্ৰেইন যুদ্ধৰ পৰা ‘ৰ’বট আর্মী’ৰ কেতবোৰ ভিডিঅ’ দেখিবলৈ পোৱা গৈছিল। এটা ভিডিঅ’ত ৰাছিয়াৰ সেনাসকলে ইউক্ৰেইনৰ এটা অস্ত্ৰসজ্জিত ৰ’বটৰ সন্মুখত আত্মসমৰ্পণ কৰা দৃশ্যও দেখা গৈছিল। ইউক্ৰেইনৰ ৰাষ্ট্ৰপতিয়েও এই ঘটনাৰ সত্যতা নিশ্চিত কৰিছিল।
ফ্ৰণ্ট লাইনত দুটা হিউমেন’য়ড ৰ’বটঃ
চলিত বৰ্ষৰ ১৩ এপ্ৰিলত ইউক্ৰেইনৰ ৰাষ্ট্ৰপতি জেলেন্সকীয়ে সামাজিক মাধ্যমত এটা পোষ্টৰ জৰিয়তে ৰ’বট আর্মীৰ এক গুৰুত্বপূৰ্ণ সফলতাৰ কথা উল্লেখ কৰে। তেওঁ কয় যে- ৰাছিয়াৰ সৈনিকসকলে ইউক্ৰেইনৰ ৰ’বট আর্মীৰ সন্মুখত আত্মসমৰ্পণ কৰিছিল। এতিয়া প্ৰথমবাৰৰ বাবে দুটা হিউমেন’য়ড ৰ’বট ইউক্ৰেইনৰ ফ্ৰণ্ট লাইনত মোতায়েন কৰা হৈছে। বৰ্তমানৰ পৰিস্থিতিয়ে এই কথাও স্পষ্ট কৰি তুলিছে যে ৰ’বট আর্মীৰ যুগত ইউক্ৰেইন যথেষ্ট আগবাঢ়ি গৈছে।
হিউমেন’য়ড ৰ’বট ‘ফেণ্টম Mk1’ৰ বিশেষত্বঃ

এই ৰ’বটটোৰ নাম ফেণ্টম Mk1 (Phantom Mk1)।  ইয়াৰ উচ্চতা প্ৰায় ৬ ফুট আৰু ওজন প্ৰায় ৮০ কিলোগ্ৰাম।
ই নিজৰ সৈতে ৪০ কিলোগ্ৰাম পৰ্যন্ত ওজন বহন কৰিব পাৰে। এই ৰ’বটটো ‘ষ্টাৰ ৱাৰ্ছ’ ছবিখনৰ বেটেল ড্ৰইডৰ দৰে দেখিবলৈ।
এই ৰ’বটসমূহ নিৰ্মাণ কৰা আমেৰিকাৰ ষ্টাৰ্টআপ কোম্পানী Foundation Roboticsয়ে আমেৰিকাৰ প্ৰতিৰক্ষা বিভাগৰ সৈতে ২৪ মিলিয়ন ডলাৰ (প্ৰায় ২০০ কোটি টকা)ৰ এক চুক্তি স্বাক্ষৰ কৰিছে
কোম্পানীটোৱে আগন্তুক কেইবছৰৰ ভিতৰত এনে হাজাৰ হাজাৰ ৰ’বট নিৰ্মাণৰ পৰিকল্পনা কৰিছে।

ইফালে, কোম্পানীটোৰ মুখ্য কাৰ্যবাহী বিষয়া সংকেত পাঠকৰ মতে- এই ৰ’বটসমূহক এনে ধৰণে ডিজাইন কৰা হৈছে যাতে সিহঁত পৰি গ’লেও পুনৰ থিয় হ’ব পাৰে, অধিক ওজন বহন কৰিব পাৰে আৰু ইহঁতৰ ওপৰত ধূলি-বালি বা পানীৰ প্ৰভাৱ সহজে নপৰে। কেৱল সেনাবাহিনীতেই নহয়, নিৰ্মাণ উদ্যোগৰ ক্ষেত্ৰতো ইয়াৰ ব্যৱহাৰৰ সম্ভাৱনা আছে।
ফেণ্টম Mk1-ৰ প্ৰত্যাহ্বানঃ
বিশেষজ্ঞসকলৰ মতে, এই প্ৰযুক্তি এতিয়াও প্ৰাৰম্ভিক পৰ্যায়ত আছে আৰু সম্পূৰ্ণ সফল হ’বলৈ প্ৰায় এটা দশকৰ সময় লাগিব পাৰে।

বেটাৰী: ফেণ্টম Mk1-ৰ বেটাৰী বৰ্তমান এক ঘণ্টাতকৈও কম সময় চলে। যদিও ইয়াৰ পৰৱৰ্তী সংস্কৰণ Mk2-ত ৬ ঘণ্টালৈকে বেটাৰী স্থায়িত্ব বৃদ্ধি কৰাৰ দাবী কৰা হৈছে।
ভাৰসাম্য ৰক্ষা: ওখ-চাপৰ পথ বা যুদ্ধক্ষেত্ৰত দুখন ভৰিৰ ওপৰত ভাৰসাম্য ৰক্ষা কৰি চলাচল কৰাটো অত্যন্ত কঠিন। চকাযুক্ত ৰ’বটৰ তুলনাত ভৰিযুক্ত ৰ’বটৰ চলাচল কৰা অধিক জটিল।
চাইবাৰ সুৰক্ষা: এই ৰ’বটসমূহে সম্পূৰ্ণ স্বতন্ত্ৰভাৱে কাম কৰিব নোৱাৰে। নিৰ্দেশ লাভৰ বাবে যোগাযোগ ব্যৱস্থাৰ ওপৰত নিৰ্ভৰ কৰিব লাগে, যাৰ ফলত হেকিং কৰাৰ আশংকা সদায় থাকেই।

যদিওবা এতিয়াও সম্পূৰ্ণৰূপে এনে হিউমেন’য়ড ৰ’বটৰ ব্যৱহাৰ সম্পূৰ্ণৰূপে কৰিব পৰা হোৱা নাই। কিন্তু তথ্য-প্ৰযুক্তিৰ বৰ্তমানৰ সময়ত সেয়া অসম্ভৱ বুলি ক’ব নোৱাৰি। নিশ্চিতভাৱে এনে দিনো আহিব যেতিয়া দেশসমূহে অত্যাধুনিক প্ৰযুক্তিৰে যুদ্ধক্ষেত্ৰত এনে ৰ’বটসমূহক ব্যৱহাৰ কৰা দেখা পোৱা যাব।

title: নগাঁৱত ৰাষ্ট্ৰীয় পৰিসংখ্যা দিৱস উদযাপন
description:
ডিজিটেল সংবাদ,নগাওঁঃ ভাৰতত প্ৰতিবছৰে ২৯ জুনত ৰাষ্ট্ৰীয় পৰিসংখ্যা দিৱস পালন কৰা হয় । দেশৰ আধুনিক পৰিসংখ্যাৰ জনক প্ৰশান্ত চন্দ্ৰ মহালানোবিশৰ জন্মদিনত এই দিৱসটো উদযাপন কৰা হয় । ভাৰতীয় পৰিসংখ্যাবিদসকলৰ অৱদানৰ ওপৰত আলোকপাত কৰিবলৈ আৰু অধ্যয়নৰ ক্ষেত্ৰ হিচাপে পৰিসংখ্যা বিজ্ঞানক জনপ্ৰিয় কৰিবলৈ এই দিৱস উদযাপন কৰা হয় ।
সমগ্ৰ দেশৰ লগতে সোমবাৰে নগাঁৱতোৰাষ্ট্ৰীয় পৰিসংখ্যা দিৱস উদযাপন কৰা হয়। নগাঁও জিলা অৰ্থনীতি আৰু পৰিসংখ্যা বিভাগৰ উপসঞ্চালকৰ কাৰ্যালয়ৰ উদ্যোগত দিনযোৰা বিভিন্ন কাৰ্যসূচীৰে এই দিৱস পালন কৰে। পুৱা পৰিৱেশ নিকাকৰণৰ বাবে বৃক্ষৰোপণ পাছতে বন্তি প্ৰজ্বলনেৰে কাৰ্যসূচীৰ শুভাৰম্ভ কৰা হয়। ইয়াৰ পাছতে ভাৰতীয় পৰিসংখ্যা বিজ্ঞানৰ পিতৃপুৰুষ প্ৰশান্ত চন্দ্ৰ মহালানোবিশৰ প্ৰতিচ্ছবিত গভীৰ শ্ৰদ্ধাঞ্জলি অৰ্পণ কৰা হয়।
এই দিৱসৰ লগত সংগতি ৰাখি বিভাগৰ বিষয়া-কৰ্মচাৰীসকলৰ সমবেত সংগীতেৰে এক বিশেষ মুকলি সভা অনুষ্ঠিত হয়। অসম চৰকাৰৰ অৰ্থনীতি আৰু পৰিসংখ্যা বিভাগৰ অতিৰিক্ত সঞ্চালক জয়ন্ত সোনোৱালৰ সভাপতিত্বত অনুষ্ঠিত এই সভাৰ উদ্দেশ্য ব্যাখ্যা কৰে নগাঁও কাৰ্যালয়ৰ ক্ষেত্ৰ সহায়ক পাৰ্থজ্যোতি দাসে।
আনফালে, নগাঁও কাৰ্যালয়ৰ পৰিদৰ্শক কৌস্তভমণি বৰাই আদৰণি ভাষণ প্ৰদান কৰা এই গুৰুত্বপূৰ্ণ সভাত নগাঁও জিলা অৰ্থনীতি আৰু পৰিসংখ্যা বিভাগৰ উপসঞ্চালক মোহন চন্দ্ৰ কোঁৱৰে দিৱসটোৰ মূল তাৎপৰ্য আৰু প্ৰাসংগিকতা ব্যাখ্যা কৰে। সভাত তেজপুৰস্থিত ৰাষ্ট্ৰীয় পৰিসংখ্যা জৰীপ সংস্থাৰ জ্যেষ্ঠ পৰিসংখ্যা বিষয়া মণিৰাজ অধিকাৰীয়ে সমল ব্যক্তি হিচাপে অংশগ্ৰহণ কৰে। তেওঁ অৰ্থনীতি আৰু পৰিসংখ্যা বিভাগৰ কাম-কাজৰ এক সম্যক বিশ্লেষণ আগবঢ়োৱাৰ লগতে দেশৰ পৰিকল্পনা আৰু উন্নয়নৰ ক্ষেত্ৰত পি চি মহলানবিছৰ জীৱন দৰ্শন তথা অৱদানৰ বিষয়ে বহলাই উল্লেখ কৰে।
এই বিশেষ উদযাপনত জিলাখনৰ কেইবাগৰাকীও জ্যেষ্ঠ বিষয়া আৰু বিশিষ্ট ব্যক্তিয়ে অংশগ্ৰহণ কৰে। ইয়াৰ ভিতৰত আছিল জিলা কৃষি বিষয়া নিখিল নাৰায়ণ বৰুৱা,নগাঁও পৌৰসভাৰ কাৰ্যবাহী বিষয়া পংকজ কুমাৰ ভূঞা,জ্যেষ্ঠ পৰিসংখ্যা বিষয়া মনদ্বীপ শুক্লবৈদ্য,অৰ্থনীতি আৰু পৰিসংখ্যা বিভাগৰ অৱসৰপ্ৰাপ্ত অতিৰিক্ত সঞ্চালক পুনাৰাম দাস,জ্যেষ্ঠ সাংবাদিকদ্বয় কনক হাজৰিকা আৰু মুনীন্দ্ৰ শইকীয়া।
ইপিনে, দেশৰ সামগ্ৰিক উন্নয়নৰ খতিয়ান আৰু সঠিক পৰিকল্পনা প্ৰস্তুত কৰাৰ ক্ষেত্ৰত পৰিসংখ্যা বিজ্ঞানৰ গুৰুত্বৰ ওপৰত আলোকপাত কৰি সভাত উপস্থিত থকা বক্তাসকলে বিভাগটোক অধিক শক্তিশালী কৰাৰ পোষকতা কৰে।

title: ശബരിമല സ്വർണക്കൊള്ള കേസ്; പി എസ് പ്രശാന്തും ഭരണസമിതിയും പ്രതികള്‍, വിശ്വാസ വഞ്ചന, വ്യാജരേഖ ചമയ്ക്കൽ എന്നീ കുറ്റങ്ങള്‍
description: 2019 ലെ ഗൂഢാലോചനയുടെ തുടർച്ചയാണ് 2025ലേതെന്ന് എസ്ഐടി പറയുന്നു. പ്രതികള്‍ക്കെതിരെ വിശ്വാസ വഞ്ചന, വ്യാജരേഖ ചമയ്ക്കൽ എന്നീ കുറ്റങ്ങളാണ് ചുമത്തിയിരിക്കുന്നത്.
content: <p><strong>തിരുവനന്തപുരം:</strong> ശബരിമല സ്വർണ്ണക്കൊള്ള കേസിൽ തിരുവിതാംകൂർ ദേവസ്വം ബോർഡ് മുൻ പ്രസിഡന്റ് പി എസ് പ്രശാന്തും ദേവസ്വം ഭരണസമിതിയെയും തന്ത്രിയെയും പ്രതിയാക്കി എസ് ഐടി ഹൈക്കോടതിയിൽ റിപ്പോർട്ട് നൽകി. 2019ൽ സ്വർണ്ണം പൊതിഞ്ഞ ദ്വാരപാലക പാളികളിൽ അളവ് കുറച്ച് സ്വർണ്ണം പൂശിയ നടപടി മറച്ച് വയ്ക്കാൻ പ്രതികൾ ക്രിമിനൽ ഗൂഢാലോചന നടത്തിയെന്നാണ് എസ് ഐ ടി കണ്ടെത്തൽ. ദേവസ്വം ചട്ടങ്ങൾ ലംഘിച്ച് ദ്വാരപാലക പാളികൾ ചെന്നൈയിലേക്ക് കൊണ്ട് പോയത് സ്പോൺസറും ദേവസ്വം ബോർഡ് ഉദ്യോഗസ്ഥരും ചേർന്ന് നടത്തിയ വർഷങ്ങൾ നീണ്ട കൃത്യമായ പദ്ധതിയിലൂടെയെന്നാണ് എസ് ഐ ടി കണ്ടെത്തൽ.</p><p>എൽഡിഎഫ് സർക്കാരിന്റെ മറ്റൊരു ഭരണസമിതി കൂടി ശബരിമല സ്വർണ്ണക്കൊള്ള കേസ് പ്രതികളാകുന്നു. ഭരണസമിതി അദ്ധ്യക്ഷൻ പിഎസ് പ്രശാന്ത്, സ്പോൺസർ ഉണ്ണികൃഷ്ണന്‍ പോറ്റി, സ്മാർട്ട് ക്രിയേഷൻസ് സിഇഒ പങ്കജ് ഭണ്ഡാരി, ബോർഡ് മുൻ അംഗം അഡ്വ എ അജികുമാര്‍, തന്ത്രി കണ്ഠരര് രാജീവര് എന്നിവരടക്കമാണ് പ്രതികളാകുന്നത്. 1998ൽ സ്വ‌ർണ്ണം കൊണ്ട് പൊതിഞ്ഞ ദ്വാരപാലക പാളികൾ. അത് 2019ൽ നിറം മങ്ങിയെന്ന കാരണം കണ്ടെത്തി ഉണ്ണികൃഷ്ണൻ പോറ്റിയുടെ നേതൃത്വത്തിൽ ചെന്നൈയിൽ കൊണ്ട് പോയി സ്വർണ്ണം പൂശുന്നു. സ്വർണ്ണം പൊതിഞ്ഞ ദ്വാരപാലകരെന്നത് ചെമ്പ് പ്ലേറ്റുകളെന്ന് രേഖപ്പെടുത്തി അനുമതി സംഘടിപ്പിച്ചത് ദേവസ്വം ഉദ്യോഗസ്ഥർ.</p><p>സ്വർണ്ണപാളി എന്നത് ഒഴിവാക്കി നേർത്ത പാളിയിൽ സ്വ‌ർണ്ണം പൂശി തിരികെ കൊണ്ട് വരുന്നു. ബാക്കി വന്ന സ്വർണ്ണം ഉണ്ണികൃഷ്ണൻ പോറ്റിയുടെ നേതൃത്വത്തിൽ കവർന്നു. 40 വർഷത്തെ വാറന്റിയുണ്ടെന്ന സർട്ടിഫിക്കറ്റ് വ്യാജമായി ഉണ്ടാക്കി. എന്നാൽ 2023ഓടെ സ്വർണ്ണം നിറം മങ്ങി. ചെമ്പ് പുറത്ത് വന്നു. ഈ കൊള്ള മറച്ചു വെക്കാൻ 2023 മുതൽ ഭരണസമിതിയുടെ അറിവോടെ ഗൂഢാലോചന തുടങ്ങി. സ്വർണ്ണം അപഹരിച്ചതായി അറിഞ്ഞിട്ടും &nbsp;പി എസ് പ്രശാന്ത് അദ്ധ്യക്ഷനായ ഭരണസമിതി ഇത് മറച്ച് വെച്ചു. 2025ൽ ദേവസ്വം മാനുവൽ ലംഘിച്ച് ഹൈക്കോടതിയുടെയോ സ്പെഷൽ കമ്മീഷണറോ അറിയാതെ ശബരിമലയിൽ നിന്ന് ചെന്നൈയിലേക്ക് ദ്വാരപാലക പാളികൾ കടത്തികൊണ്ട് പോയി.&nbsp;</p><p>സ്വർണം കവർന്നത് കൊണ്ടാണ് പാളികൾക്ക് മങ്ങൽ ഉണ്ടായത്. അത് ഒളിച്ചു വെക്കാനായിരുന്നു വീണ്ടും സ്വർണം പൂശൽ. മുൻ കവർച്ച മറച്ചു വെക്കാനുള്ള ഈ ശ്രമത്തിൽ ക്രിമിനൽ ഗൂഢാലോചന ഉണ്ടെന്നും എസ് ഐടി കണ്ടെത്തി. തുടർന്നാണ് എൽഡിഎഫ് സർക്കാർ നിയമിച്ച പി എസ് പ്രശാന്ത് ഭരണസമിതിയും പ്രതിപ്പട്ടികയിൽ ഉൾപ്പെട്ടത്. ഇവരെ കൂടാതെ ദേവസ്വം ബോർഡ് മുൻ അംഗം സുന്ദരേശൻ, ദേവസ്വം ബോർഡ് സെക്രട്ടറി ബിന്ദു, തന്ത്രി മഹേഷ് മോഹനര്, മുൻ തിരുവാഭരണം കമ്മീഷണർ സുനില, മുൻ എക്സിക്യൂട്ടീവ് ഓഫീസർമാരായ ഒ.ജി. ബിജു, വി.എസ്. രാജേന്ദ്രപ്രസാദ്, അഡ്മിനിസ്ട്രേറ്റീവ് ഓഫീസർ ശ്രീനിവാസ്, അസിസ്റ്റന്റ് എക്സിക്യൂട്ടീവ് ഓഫീസർ ഹേമന്ത്, ബെല്ലാരിയിലെ സ്വർണ്ണവ്യാപാരി ഗോവർദ്ധൻ എന്നിവരുടെ പങ്കും അന്വേഷണ പരിധിയിൽ ഉണ്ടെന്ന് കോടതിയിൽ എസ് ഐ ടി അറിയിച്ചു. ശാസ്ത്രീയ പരിശോധന ഫലങ്ങൾ കിട്ടാൻ ഇനിയും സമയമെടുക്കും എന്നതിനാൽ കുറ്റപത്രം നൽകാൻ കൂടുതൽ സമയം നൽകണമെന്നും എസ് ഐ ടി ആവശ്യപ്പെട്ടു.കേസ് വരുന്ന 20ആം തിയതി വീണ്ടും പരിഗണിക്കും.</p><p></p><p>&nbsp;</p>

title: 23 Hospitalised After Eating Snacks From Local Stall In Andhra Village
description: Doctors urged the public to avoid consuming food from unhygienic sources.
content: The affected persons developed vomiting, diarrhoea, abdominal pain and fever within hours of eating snacks sold at a local roadside stall.

title: ୫ ବର୍ଷ ଦୂରର କଥା….ବିଜୟଙ୍କ ଉପରେ ବର୍ଷିଲେ ଷ୍ଟାଲିନ, ୩ରୁ୬ ମାସରେ ଖେଳ ଶେଷ
description:
ଚେନ୍ନାଇ,୨୯।୬: ତାମିଲନାଡୁ ରାଜନୀତିରେ ବୟାନବାଜି ତୀବ୍ର ହୋଇଛି । ରବିବାର ଦିନ ଡିଏମକେ ମୁଖ୍ୟ ଏମ୍ କେ ଷ୍ଟାଲିନ୍ ବିଜୟଙ୍କ ନେତୃତ୍ୱାଧୀନ ସରକାରର ସ୍ଥିରତା ଉପରେ ପ୍ରଶ୍ନ ଉଠାଇଛନ୍ତି । ସେ କହିଛନ୍ତି ଯେ, ସରକାର ପାଞ୍ଚ ବର୍ଷର କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକାଳ ପୂରଣ କରିପାରିବ ନାହିଁ। ଚେନ୍ନାଇରେ ଏକ ଡିଏମକେ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକ୍ରମରେ ଭାଷଣ ଦେବା ସମୟରେ – ଯେଉଁଠାରେ ବିଭିନ୍ନ ରାଜନୈତିକ ଦଳର ୫,୦୦୦ ରୁ ଅଧିକ ନେତା ଏବଂ କର୍ମୀ ଦଳରେ ଯୋଗ ଦେଇଥିଲେ । ଷ୍ଟାଲିନ୍ […]
The post ୫ ବର୍ଷ ଦୂରର କଥା….ବିଜୟଙ୍କ ଉପରେ ବର୍ଷିଲେ ଷ୍ଟାଲିନ, ୩ରୁ୬ ମାସରେ ଖେଳ ଶେଷ appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.

title: How Akshay's Rustom crushed Soni's Nanavati directorial dream
description: Soni Razdan's second directorial 'Love Affair', based on the Nanavati murder case and starring Ali Fazal and Kalki Koechlin, was shelved just three weeks before shooting began. Akshay Kumar's 'Rustom', based on the same case, further derailed her plans. However, Soni remains hopeful, saying "one day, when the time is right, it'll happen."

title: Digilocker CBSE Result 2026: Class 10 second board result expected this week; how to check via DigiLocker and other platforms
description: CBSE Class 10th second board result: Central Board of Secondary Education is all set to release class 10th second board exam result soon.
content: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) is expected to release the Class 10 Second Board Examination Result 2026 shortly. More than 6.68 lakh students appeared for this exam, which gives them a chance to improve their marks. The results have not been declared yet, but they may be available soon, possibly today or tomorrow.This is part of CBSE’s new two-exam system, students who want to better their performance can take a second board exam in the same year instead of waiting for the next academic session. After the recent announcement of Class 12 revaluation results, attention has now turned to the Class 10 second board outcomes.Once declared, students will be able to check their results on several platforms. These include the official CBSE websites (cbse.gov.in and results.cbse.nic.in), DigiLocker, the UMANG app, SMS service, and Interactive Voice Response System (IVRS). Having multiple options helps manage heavy traffic on the websites when results are released.How to Check Results on DigiLocker:  Here are the simple steps:Open the DigiLocker website or mobile app.Log in with your registered mobile number or other details.Search for the CBSE Class 10 result or marksheet section.Enter your roll number and other required information.Download the digital scorecard once it appears.Students should keep their roll number, date of birth, and admit card details ready.How to check on official CBSE websites:Visit cbse.gov.in or results.cbse.nic.in.Look for the link for “CBSE Class 10 Second Board Result 2026”.Enter your roll number and other details.Submit the information to view the scorecard.Download and save the provisional marksheet. What the Scorecard ContainsThe provisional marksheet will include the student’s name, roll number, date of birth, father’s name, subject-wise theory and practical marks (where applicable), total marks, maximum marks, overall grade, and pass or fail status. Students should check all details carefully. Any errors can be corrected by contacting the school or CBSE officials.The second board exam system aims to reduce stress and provide better opportunities for students. Results will also be available through the UMANG app and other services. Students are advised to use official platforms only and avoid unverified websites. Keep your login details ready and check the official CBSE websites regularly for the exact result date and link. Further updates will be shared by CBSE as soon as the results are ready.

title: Ajay Devgn’s ‘Chauhaan’ in TROUBLE; Kshatriya Parishad condemns
description: Ajay Devgn's 'Chauhaan' faces fresh controversy as the Kshatriya Parishad accuses filmmakers of misusing Rajput history and identity for political gain. The organization decries the 'weaponisation of Rajput history,' asserting that the Chauhan legacy belongs to history, not partisan campaigns. This follows earlier criticism of the teaser's portrayal of the Kashmir conflict and a dialogue about pellet guns.

title: The Coral announce 2026 UK and Ireland tour with Dave McCabe, The Mary Wallopers and more
description:
Their first new tour dates in nearly three years come following the surprise release of new album ‘388’ earlier this year
The post The Coral announce 2026 UK and Ireland tour with Dave McCabe, The Mary Wallopers and more appeared first on NME.
content:
The Coral have announced their first UK and Ireland tour in nearly three years, with support coming from Dave McCabe, The Mary Wallopers and more. Find ticket information and a full list of new dates below.

READ MORE: The Coral on going “back to basics” with surprise new album ‘388’: “We’ve rediscovered an attitude of ‘Fuck ’em’”


The new tour dates are announced today (Monday June 29) and come in celebration of the indie icons surprise releasing new album ‘388’ last month.
The 11-track LP was inspired by their formative years, recorded to an original 1970’s Tascam 388 tape recorder, and captured frontman James Skelly and co. in their rawest form – keeping the analogue sound from the studio and subtle mistakes made along the way.
It was released with a no-fuss approach, stocked unannounced at independent record shops shelves in just one standard black vinyl format.
Now, to celebrate the new album, The Coral are embarking on their first headline UK tour in almost three years.

  










View this post on Instagram

























Dates kick off in Sheffield on October 2, and continue with stops in Newcastle, Leeds, London, Brighton, Manchester and Birmingham throughout the month. Dates wrap up with shows at Dublin’s 3Arena and Glasgow’s OVO Hydro on December 10 and 18 respectively.
Support comes from The Zutons‘ Dave McCabe, Mozart Estate and The Mary Wallopers, and tickets go on sale on Friday (July 3) at 9:30am local time. Visit here for tickets.
The Coral’s ‘388’ UK tour dates are:
OCTOBER
2 – Sheffield, Foundry*
9– Newcastle, Boiler Shop**
10 – Leeds, Project House**
16 – London, KOKO**
17 – Brighton, Centre***
23 – Manchester, Albert Hall**
24 – Birmingham, O2 Institute 2**
DECEMBER
10 – Dublin, 3Arena***
18 – Glasgow, Ovo Hydro***
* supported by Dave McCabe
** supported by Mozart Estate
*** with The Mary Wallopers
The Coral have been regularly playing at festivals since they dropped their last album ‘Sea Of Mirrors’ in 2024, but these are their first headline tour dates since December that year.
As well as announcing the new live shows, James Skelly and co. have also released a new music video for the optimistic single ‘Leave It In The Past’, which is directed by James Slater and finds them in Birkenhead Market.
“Ian [Skelly, drummer] and I used to live above a pub in Charing Cross, Birkenhead and wander round the market at weekends,” said the frontman about the choice of location.
“It was a magical place to us. The atmosphere of the place, and the characters you still find there, reflect the ethos of ‘388’. It’s simultaneously of today and another time entirely.”

Shortly after ‘388’ arrived in record stores without prior warning, the five-piece spoke to NME about why they wanted to surprise fans with a new full-length effort.
“Releasing an album this way keeps us interested and keeps the fans interested too,” said the singer. “The idea was to release it how records used to be released: people can just have it for a couple of weeks, and it isn’t explained to anyone.”
Keyboardist Nick Power added: “Half the idea was that people would see it and think that, because we hadn’t announced anything, that ‘388’ might be a bootleg. We wanted people to wonder: ‘What is this?’ Not saying anything about it since it’s been out has been the hardest aspect.”
The post The Coral announce 2026 UK and Ireland tour with Dave McCabe, The Mary Wallopers and more appeared first on NME.

title: ଆଜିଠୁ ୪ ରାଶିର ଖୋଲୁଛି ଭାଗ୍ୟ! ବ୍ୟବସାୟ-ଇନକମ୍ ଏବଂ ଚାକିରି କ୍ଷେତ୍ରରେ ଡବଲ୍ ଲାଭ…

The post ଆଜିଠୁ ୪ ରାଶିର ଖୋଲୁଛି ଭାଗ୍ୟ! ବ୍ୟବସାୟ-ଇନକମ୍ ଏବଂ ଚାକିରି କ୍ଷେତ୍ରରେ ଡବଲ୍ ଲାଭ… appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.
content:
ହିନ୍ଦୁ ଧର୍ମ ଏବଂ ଜ୍ୟୋତିଷ ଶାସ୍ତ୍ରରେ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମାକୁ ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ଶୁଭ ଭାବରେ ବର୍ଣ୍ଣନା କରାଯାଇଛି । ଏହା ସହିତ ଜ୍ୟେଷ୍ଠ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମାକୁ ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ସ୍ବତନ୍ତ୍ର ଭାବରେ ବିବେଚନା କରାଯାଇଛି । ଜ୍ୟେଷ୍ଠ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ମା ଲକ୍ଷ୍ଣୀଙ୍କ ପାଇଁ ସମର୍ପିତ । ତେଣୁ ଆଜିର ଦିନକୁ ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ସ୍ବତନ୍ତ୍ର ଭାବରେ ବିବେଚନା କରାଯାଏ । ଜ୍ୟୋତିଷ ଶସ୍ତ୍ର ଅନୁଯାୟୀ, ଆଜି ଗ୍ରହ ନକ୍ଷେତ୍ର ମାନଙ୍କର ଏପରି ସଂଯୋଗ ଘଟୁଛି ଯାହା କିଛି ରାଶି ପାଇଁ ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ଶୁଭ ସାବ୍ୟସ୍ତ ହେବ । ବିଶେଷ କରି ୪ଟି ରାଶି ପାଇଁ ଆଜି ଦିନଟି ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ସୁଖ, ସମୃଦ୍ଧିରେ କଟିବ । ତେବେ ଚାଲନ୍ତୁ ଜାଣିବା ଉକ୍ତ ରାଶି ବିଷୟରେ ।
ମେଷ ରାଶିର ବ୍ୟକ୍ତି ବିଶେଷଙ୍କ ପାଇଁ ଆଜି ଦିନଟି କ୍ୟାରିୟରରେ ଏକ ନୂଆ ମୋଡ ଆସିବ । କିଛି ଲୋକଙ୍କୁ ନୂଆ ଚାକିରିର ଅଫର ମଧ୍ୟ ଆସିବ । ସେହିପରି ଆଉ କିଛି ଲୋକଙ୍କର ଇନ୍‌କମ୍‌ରେ ମଧ୍ୟ ସୁଧାର ଆସିବ । ଯେଉଁମାନେ ବ୍ୟବସାୟ କରୁଛନ୍ତୁ ତାଙ୍କୁ ଏକ ନୂଆ ଏବଂ ଜବରଦସ୍ତ ଡିଲ୍‌ ମିଳିବ ।
ମିଥୁନ ରାଶିର ଲୋକଙ୍କ ପାଇଁ ଆଜି ଦିନଟି ଆର୍ଥିକ ଦୃଷ୍ଟିରୁ ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ଶୁଭ ସାବ୍ୟସ୍ତ ହେବ । ଅନେକ ଦିନରୁ ପଡି ରହିଥିବା କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ସମାପ୍ତ ହେବ । ଆଜି ଆପଣ ପରିପାର ଏବଂ ପ୍ରିୟଜନଙ୍କ ସହିତ ଅଧିକା ସମୟ ବିତାଇବେ । ଯଦି ଆପଣ ଭବିଷ୍ୟତ ପାଇଁ କିଛି ଟଙ୍କା ନିବେଶ କରିବାକୁ ଯୋଜନା କରୁଛନ୍ତି ତେବେ ଏହି ସମୟ ଅତ୍ୟନ୍ତ ଶୁଭ ସାବ୍ୟସ୍ତ ହୋଇପାରିବ ।
ଧନୁ ରାଶିର ବ୍ୟକ୍ତି ବିଶେଷଙ୍କ ଜୀବନରେ ଏକ ନୂଆ ମୋଡ ଆସିବ ଯାହାକି ଆପଣଙ୍କ ଭାଗ୍ୟ ବଦଳାଇଦେବ । କିଛି କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକୁ ନେଇ ଅନେକ ଦିନ ଚିନ୍ତିରେ ଥିଲେ ଏବେ ତାହା ପୂରଣ ହୋଇଯିବ । କିଛି ଲୋକଙ୍କୁ ନୂଆ ଚାକିରି ମିଳିବାର ମଧ୍ୟ ସମ୍ଭାବନା ଅଛି ।
ତୁଳା ରାଶିର ବ୍ୟକ୍ତି ମାନଙ୍କ ପାଇଁ ମଧ୍ୟ ଜ୍ୟୋଷ୍ଠ ପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣିମା ବହୁତ ଶୁଭ ସାବ୍ୟସ୍ତ ହେବ । ଲକ୍ଷ୍ମୀ ଆପଣଙ୍କ ଉପରେ ପ୍ରସନ୍ନ ହୋଇଯିବେ । ଅଚାନାକ ଅର୍ଥ ପ୍ରାପ୍ତି ହେବ । କର୍ଜରୁ ମୁକ୍ତି ମିଳିବ । ନୂଆ କାମର ଆରମ୍ଭ ହେବ ।
The post ଆଜିଠୁ ୪ ରାଶିର ଖୋଲୁଛି ଭାଗ୍ୟ! ବ୍ୟବସାୟ-ଇନକମ୍ ଏବଂ ଚାକିରି କ୍ଷେତ୍ରରେ ଡବଲ୍ ଲାଭ… appeared first on Dharitri Odia News.

title: Axis Bank CFO Puneet Sharma resigns
‘House Of The Dragon’ viewers laud “fucking poetic” character death in latest episode
description:
"The highlight of my life"
The post ‘House Of The Dragon’ viewers laud “fucking poetic” character death in latest episode appeared first on NME.
content:
**Major spoilers for House Of The Dragon season three episode two below**
House Of The Dragon has killed off another major character in season three following a spate of deaths in the recent premiere.
The Game Of Thrones prequel returned for its third run last weekend, showcasing the Battle Of The Gullet – touted as the bloodiest sea battle in Westeros history.
The episode also saw the demise of Jace Velaryon (Harry Collett), the son of Rhaenyra Targaryen (Emma D’Arcy), and dragon Vermax, as well as Sharako Lohar (Abigail Thorn), Jason Lannister (Jefferson Hall) and likely Tyland Lannister (Jefferson Hall).

However, this weekend’s (Sunday June 28) follow-up featured another big death, which came after Rhaenyra and Daemon (Matt Smith) took King’s Landing while Aemond Targaryen (Ewan Mitchell) was convinced by Alicent Hightower (Olivia Cooke) to go to Harrenhal.
However, she was captured, while her father Otto (Rhys Ifans) – who had been held prisoner in the Red Keep – was offered up as a gift to Daemon. He pushed Rhaenyra to behead Otto, which she tearfully did after a few hacks, before Alicent arrived to see his bloody corpse on the floor.
Otto’s death has sparked many reactions on social media, many fans pleased to see the back of the character after three seasons.
“I literally started the episode crying then ending smiling Otto BEEN needed that!!!” one wrote on X. “He literally started all this bs and then his dumbass daughter doubled down and made it worse!”
“Yes!!!! Otto is GONE,” another beamed, a third adding: “Seeing Otto get beheaded was the highlight of my life. That bitch was the reason for all of Alicent’s suffering and I’m glad he’s dead. I will miss him serving cunt though.”
“The main responsible for the war. Forced Alicent into marrying the king. Manipulated and took advantage of Viserys selfishly to put his own line on the throne. Usurped Rhaenyra and planned to have her killed. Her being the one to finally take him down was FUCKING POETIC,” a fourth said.
However, some others were less enthusiastic, one sharing: “As a show viewer, Otto deserved being beheaded however he will be missed as a great character who was well played and embraced by Rhys Ifans.”
A further person mused: “I gotta say i didn’t like Otto’s death…. Such a good character to die in such a sloppy way.”

Last week, fans also shared their reactions to a “gross” mother-son kiss between Alicent and Aemond amid a tense conversation, one viewer admitting: “That was easily the most uncomfortable kiss I’ve ever seen on TV.”
Elsewhere, viewership of House Of The Dragon season three is down from season two, the first episode racking up 21.5million viewers across three days on HBO and HBO Max as compared to the previous season’s 23.4million viewers.
Season three consists of eight episodes, which will air through to August on HBO Max, Sky Atlantic and NOW TV. Beyond that, the series will run for two more seasons, concluding its run with season five.

title: Bajaj Auto Buyback: Record Rs 5633 crore buyback opens on July 1 - What investors need to know
description: Bajaj Auto Buyback: The buyback, approved by the board and shareholders, underscores the company’s strong cash position and commitment to enhancing shareholder value.​
content: Bajaj Auto Buyback: Bajaj Auto Ltd has announced its largest-ever buyback, offering to repurchase up to 4,69,4000 shares at a price of Rs 12,000 per share, aggregating approximately Rs 5,632.80 crore.The buyback, approved by the board and shareholders, underscores the company’s strong cash position and commitment to enhancing shareholder value.Bajaj Auto BuybackThe tender offer route buyback carries a significant 23 per cent premium to the record date closing price of Rs 9,750 per share. This marks a notable return of capital to shareholders, especially when compared to the company’s previous buybacks in 2024 (Rs 4,000 crore at Rs 10,000 per share, a 43 per cent premium) and 2022 (Rs 2,500 crore via open market).Bajaj Auto Buyback: Key DetailsRecord Date: June 24, 2026Buyback Opens: July 1, 2026Buyback Closes: July 7, 2026Settlement By: July 14, 2026The buyback will be funded entirely from the company’s free reserves. Promoters are not participating in the offer, which benefits public shareholders by increasing their proportional allocation.Also Read | Lenskart Block Deal: Rs 1944 crore share sale planned at 2.8% discount; Check detailsFinancial ImpactThe buyback is expected to deliver immediate accretive benefits to the company’s financial metrics.EPS Improvement: Standalone basic EPS is projected to rise from Rs 352 to Rs 358 post-buyback.ROE Enhancement: Return on Net Worth (RONW) will improve from 29.3 per cent to 32.0 per cent.Promoter Holding Increase: Promoter stake will rise from 55.01 per cent to 55.95 per cent purely due to the reduction in total outstanding shares, with no additional purchases by promoters.Bajaj Auto share priceBajaj Auto shares fell over 7.61 per cent in one month. The stock overall gained nearly 7 per cent in 2026 so far. The stock jumped 15.38 per cent over the past year.Also Read | Bajaj Auto Buyback: Record date set for June 24 ahead of Rs 5,632 crore payout(Disclaimer: The above article is meant for informational purposes only, and should not be considered as any investment advice. ET NOW DIGITAL suggests its readers/audience to consult their financial advisors before making any money related decisions.)

title: SIA files 737-page chargesheet against Yasin Malik, 4 others in Sarla Bhat murder case
description:
Srinagar, June 29: The State Investigation Agency (SIA), Kashmir on Monday filed a comprehensive 737-page chargesheet before a Special Court in Srinagar in connection with the 1990 abduction and killing of SKIMS staff nurse case. This marks a significant development in the investigation of a decades-old terror-related case pertains to the abduction and murder of Sarla Bhat, who was employed as a nurse at Sher-i-Kashmir Institute of Medical Sciences (SKIMS) Soura, and was killed on April 18, 1990.
The chargesheet, as per the officials has been submitted before the Court of the Additional Sessions Judge, TADA/POTA and Special Judge designated under the NIA Act, Srinagar. The investigation was transferred to the SIA by the Director General of Police, Jammu and Kashmir, on March 18, 2024, following which the agency carried out a detailed probe.
Officials said the chargesheet is based on oral testimonies, documentary records, forensic and ballistic reports, medical evidence, electronic material and extensive field investigations collected and analysed during the course of the investigation.
The SIA stated that filing the chargesheet nearly 35 years after the incident marks an important step in pursuing justice in one of the Valley’s long-pending terror cases. The agency said the investigation demonstrates that serious terror-related offences continue to be investigated irrespective of the passage of time.
According to the investigation, Sarla Bhat was allegedly abducted near SKIMS on April 18, 1990, subjected to torture and later shot dead in the Omer Colony area of Malbagh, Srinagar. The agency said the case remained unresolved for decades due to the prevailing security situation and an atmosphere of fear that discouraged witnesses from coming forward.
Following the reopening of the case, investigators reconstructed the sequence of events with the help of protected witnesses, eyewitness accounts, forensic analysis, medical records and documentary evidence, the SIA said.
The chargesheet names former JKLF chief Mohammad Yasin Malik along with Khurshid Ahmad Chalkoo, Abdul Hamid Sheikh, Mohammad Yousuf Sofi alias Idrees and Ghulam Mohammad Taploo as accused in the case. According to the agency, three of the accused—Abdul Hamid Sheikh, Mohammad Yousuf Sofi alias Idrees and Ghulam Mohammad Taploo—have since died, while Mohammad Yasin Malik is currently in judicial custody in another case.
The SIA further stated that proclamation proceedings have been initiated against Khurshid Ahmad Chalkoo, who is alleged to have fled to Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir.
According to the chargesheet, offences have been invoked under Sections 364, 341, 302 read with 34, 201 and 120-B of the Ranbir Penal Code (RPC), relevant provisions of the Terrorist and Disruptive Activities (Prevention) Act (TADA), 1987, and Sections 7 and 27 of the Indian Arms Act, 1959.
The investigation also states that allegations portraying Sarla Bhat as an informer were found to be baseless and were allegedly used as a pretext for her targeted killing. The agency has alleged that the murder formed part of a broader campaign intended to spread fear and facilitate the displacement of members of the Kashmiri Pandit community during the early years of militancy.
The SIA said the filing of the chargesheet reflects its continued commitment to investigating unresolved terror cases and bringing those allegedly involved before the judicial process. The agency maintained that no terror-related offence is beyond investigation and reaffirmed its resolve to pursue such cases in accordance with law.

title: Nashik Police News :  नाशिकला तीन नवे ‘एसीपी’; आज होणार हजर, अनुभवी अधिकाऱ्यांना मुदतवाढ
description: नाशिक | प्रतिनिधी | Nashik सिंहस्थाचा पूर्वानुभव लक्षात घेता शहरातील तीन सहाय्यक पोलीस आयुक्तांना (एसीपी) मुदतवाढ (Extension) देण्यात आली असून, इतर विभागांतून आणखी तिघांची नाशिक शहरात नियुक्ती करण्यात आली आहे. हे नवनियुक्त सहाय्यक आयुक्त सोमवारी (दि. २९) आयुक्तालयात हजर होण्याची शक्यता आहे. सिंहस्थ कुंभमेळ्यासारखा अत्यंत महत्त्वाचा आणि मोठा सोहळा शांततेत पार पाडण्यासाठी नाशिकमध्ये (Nashik) अनुभवी […]
content:
नाशिक | प्रतिनिधी | Nashik



सिंहस्थाचा पूर्वानुभव लक्षात घेता शहरातील तीन सहाय्यक पोलीस आयुक्तांना (एसीपी) मुदतवाढ (Extension) देण्यात आली असून, इतर विभागांतून आणखी तिघांची नाशिक शहरात नियुक्ती करण्यात आली आहे. हे नवनियुक्त सहाय्यक आयुक्त सोमवारी (दि. २९) आयुक्तालयात हजर होण्याची शक्यता आहे. सिंहस्थ कुंभमेळ्यासारखा अत्यंत महत्त्वाचा आणि मोठा सोहळा शांततेत पार पाडण्यासाठी नाशिकमध्ये (Nashik) अनुभवी फौज तैनात ठेवण्यावर प्रशासनाचा भर आहे.



राज्य गृह विभागाच्या आदेशानुसार, नाशिक शहर आणि ग्रामीण पोलीस दलातील (Rural Police Force) काही वरिष्ठ अधिकाऱ्यांना बदल्यांसाठी एक वर्षाची मुदतवाढ देण्यात आली आहे. याशिवाय, नाशिकचे पोलीस आयुक्त यांनी शहरातील २५० पेक्षा जास्त पोलीस अंमलदार आणि ६० पेक्षा जास्त अधिकाऱ्यांना वर्षभरासाठी मुदतवाढीचे ‘बक्षीस’ दिले आहे. यामुळे सिंहस्थाच्या काळात नाशिकचा एकूणच पोलीस बंदोबस्त मजबूत आणि अनुभवी अधिकाऱ्यांच्या नियंत्रणात राहणार आहे.



शहरातील वरिष्ठ पोलीस निरीक्षक सोहन माछरे यांची पदोन्नतीने शहरातच ‘एसीपी’ पदावर नियुक्ती झाली होती. मात्र, गृह गृह विभागाने विभागाने (Home Department) सुधारित आदेश जारी केल्याने माछरे यांची बुलढाणा येथे बदली झाली असून, त्यांच्या जागी आता प्रमोद वाघ यांची वर्णी लागली आहे. तर, शहरात सहाय्यक आयुक्त म्हणून नियुक्त झालेल्या तिघांपैकी दोघे थेट ‘एसीपी’ असून, एकजण पदोन्नतीने ‘एसीपी’ झाले आहेत. सध्या



निकम यांना ‘साइड पोस्टिंग’ मिळणार ?



सध्या नाशिकरोड विभागात कार्यरत असलेल्या सहाय्यक आयुक्त संगीता निकम या लवकरच सेवानिवृत्त होणार आहेत. त्यामुळे सिंहस्थाच्या पूर्वतयारीच्या दृष्टीने त्यांना ‘साइड पोस्टिंग’ देऊन, नवनियुक्त तिन्ही अधिकाऱ्यांना मुख्य प्रवाहातील (महत्त्वाचा) पदभार दिला जाणार का, याकडे पोलीस आयुक्तालयाचे लक्ष वेधले गेले आहे.



‘अपेक्षित पोस्टिंग’ कोणती?



नाशिक पोलीस दलात मुख्यालय, सरकारवाडा आणि विशेष शाखा ही महत्त्वाची पदे रिक्त आहेत. त्यामुळे या नवनियुक्त अधिकाऱ्यांना सिंहस्थापूर्वी कोणती ‘अपेक्षित पोस्टिंग’ मिळते, याकडे सर्वांचे लक्ष लागले आहे.



नवनियुक्त अधिकारी



१. माधव रेड्डी: नाशिक लाचलुचपत प्रतिबंधक विभागाचे (एसीबी) अपर अधीक्षक.२. पूनम पाटीलः अकोला येथील उपअधीक्षक.३. प्रमोद वाघः वरिष्ठ पोलीस निरीक्षक पदावरून पदोन्नती



सद्यस्थितीतील पदभार



शेखर देशमुख – पंचवटी विभाग, सरकारवाडा (अतिरिक्त)डॉ. सचिन बारी – अंबड विभागसंदीप मिटके – गुन्हे, विशेष शाखा (अतिरिक्त), मुख्यालय (अतिरिक्त)अद्विता शिंदे – वाहतूक विभागसंगीता निकम – नाशिकरोड विभाग

title: Stock Market Closing on Monday, June 29: Sensex down 372 points, Nifty below 23,950 - Key reasons behind the fall
description: Stock Market Closing: Indian benchmark indices opened flat amid rising crude prices triggered by renewed US-Iran tensions. Selling pressure persisted throughout the session, with the Sensex closing over 400 points lower and the Nifty ending in the red.
content: Stock Market Closing: Indian equity benchmarks had a subdued start to the session as crude oil prices climbed following renewed military tensions between the US and Iran over the weekend. After opening largely flat, both the Sensex and Nifty remained under pressure through the day, with the Sensex ending nearly 400 points lower.The BSE Sensex, after hitting an intraday low of 76,621.75, ended the session at 76,728.37, down 372.10 points or 0.48 per cent. The NSE Nifty50 settled at 23,946.25, down 109.75 points or 0.46 per cent. During the day Nifty50 swung between 24,120.00 and 23,924.55. Sensex and Nifty opened almost today. The BSE Sensex started at 77,055.21, while the Nifty 50 opened at 24,061.75.From the Sensex pack, Kotak Mahindra Bank declined 3.24 per cent after the firm said its MD and CEO, Ashok Vaswani, will quit upon completion of his three-year tenure and will not seek re-appointment when his current term ends on December 31, 2026.Mahindra & Mahindra, Maruti, InterGlobe Aviation, UltraTech Cement and Larsen & Toubro were also among the laggards. In contrast, Eternal, Trent, Bharat Electronics and NTPC were among the gainers.Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, jumped 1.51 per cent to $73.09 per barrel, according to PTI."Geopolitical tensions escalated sharply over the weekend as the US and Iran exchanged attacks over the Strait of Hormuz before agreeing to halt hostilities and renew talks, keeping losses in check, but raising serious questions over the deal's durability and heightening the risk of a breakdown, leaving headline risk and volatility deeply embedded in market reactions," PTI quoted Ponmudi R, CEO of Enrich Money, an online trading and wealth tech firm.In Asian markets, South Korea's Kospi settled lower, while Japan's Nikkei 225 index, Shanghai's SSE Composite index and Hong Kong's Hang Seng index ended in positive territory.Markets in Europe were trading lower in mid-session deals. US markets ended marginally lower on Friday. Stock markets were closed on Friday for Muharram.Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) bought equities worth Rs 383.76 crore on Thursday, according to exchange data. On Thursday, the Sensex settled 109.25 points, or 0.14 per cent, higher at 77,100.47. The Nifty rose by 34.35 points, or 0.14 per cent, to end at 24,056.(Disclaimer: The above article is meant for informational purposes only, and should not be considered as any investment advice. ET NOW DIGITAL suggests its readers/audience to consult their financial advisors before making any money related decisions.)

title: Ram temple scam: 8 accused get 14-day custody
As JPMorgan’s CEO race heats up, the case for a two-person succession contest is put to the test
description: JPMorgan's CEO succession race gives a clue as to why boards still pit top contenders against each other.
content:
Companies say they want collaboration. Then comes CEO succession, when the rules seem to change.



JPMorgan Chase last week elevated Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh to co-presidents, setting up a direct contest to succeed CEO Jamie Dimon. In doing so, the bank embraced a strategy that many of the world’s biggest companies still rely on despite its risks.



While a head-to-head succession race gives directors a clearer view of who is best equipped to run a company, it can also reshape relationships at the highest levels of the organization long before a CEO is chosen.



For directors, the appeal is straightforward. Running a global company requires earning the confidence of investors, regulators, employees, and customers while making decisions across the entire business. A succession race allows boards to watch finalists lead different divisions, navigate crises, and build support across the organization.



JPMorgan’s latest reshuffle follows that playbook. Rohrbaugh, who spent much of his career in trading and investment banking, will now lead the consumer bank, giving him experience overseeing the lender’s largest business.



Petno will take sole control of the commercial and investment bank. Rather than signaling a winner, the moves broaden each executive’s résumé and give the board a richer basis for comparison over the next several years.



This approach has a long pedigree. General Electric famously put several executives through an extended succession contest before selecting Jeff Immelt to succeed Jack Welch in 2001. While the process helped identify a successor with broad operating experience, it also sent several accomplished finalists on to chief executive roles elsewhere.



Disney’s first CEO succession under Bob Iger offers a similar lesson. By the time Bob Chapek was appointed in 2020, leading contenders Thomas Staggs and Kevin Mayer had already departed. JP Morgan is already seeing that dynamic play out with the retirement announcement of Marianne Lake, CEO of Consumer & Community Banking, who has long been viewed as a leading contender to succeed Dimon. Her departure underscores how quickly a succession field can narrow once the board’s preferred direction becomes clear.



That tension explains why CEO succession remains one of a board’s most difficult responsibilities. Name an heir too early, and the company loses flexibility if circumstances change. Keep multiple candidates in the running, and the board gains more information, but risks losing the very executives it spent years developing.



JPMorgan’s latest moves suggest its board believes that tradeoff is worth making. The next few years will determine whether the competition produces the strongest possible successor to Dimon or is another reminder that even the best-designed succession races rarely end with every contender staying in the fold.



Ruth Umohruth.umoh@fortune.com
This story was originally featured on Fortune.com

title: 'No discrepancies were found'; Rajesh Exports clarifies position following ED search operations - DETAILS
description: Rajesh Exports has said it fully cooperated with the Enforcement Directorate during recent search operations, providing all requested information. The company stated no discrepancies were found in its inventory or cash.
content: Rajesh Exports has stated that it extended full cooperation to the Enforcement Directorate (ED) during the recent search operations conducted by the agency. The company said its officials provided all information and documents sought by the investigating authorities throughout the process.According to the company, the search proceedings concluded on June 25. Rajesh Exports further noted that the ED's verification did not reveal any discrepancies in the company's inventory or cash holdings.The company reiterated that it remained transparent and cooperative during the exercise, ensuring that all requests from the authorities were addressed promptly. Rajesh Exports added that the search operation was completed without any irregularities being identified in its physical stock or cash records.

title: Financial changes from July 1, 2026: 6 key updates; ITR deadline, Aadhaar, passport fees, SBI & HDFC Bank credit cards - Full details
description: Financial Changes From July 1, 2026: With the new month comes new changes. Therefore, prepare yourself for significant financial shifts starting July 1, 2026. From the ITR deadline, Aadhaar, passport fees, to SBI & HDFC Bank credit cards. Take a look at the key updates to take place starting July 1, 2026. Here’s what you must know.
content: Financial Changes From July 1, 2026: Several significant financial changes are set to take effect on July 1, 2026. These changes may impact taxpayers, bank customers, credit card users, passport applicants and Aadhaar card holders. Here's a look at the key financial changes coming into effect in July 2026. Take a look.Read more: 8th Pay Commission Salary Calculator: Rs 21 lakh arrears for level 10 central govt employees? Check full salary calculationFinancial changes from July 1, 2026: 6 key updates1. Passport Fee Hike: Centre to raise charges from July 1The Centre has revised passport application fees, with the new charges coming into effect from July 1 under the Passports (Amendment) Rules, 2026.According to the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), a fresh ordinary passport or reissue of a 36-page passport will now cost Rs 2,500, while applicants opting for a 60-page passport will have to pay Rs 3,500.The revised fee structure also covers applications under the Tatkaal scheme. From July 1, a 36-page passport issued through Tatkaal will cost Rs 5,000, while a 60-page passport will be priced at Rs 6,000.Under the Tatkaal scheme, the fee for a replacement 36-page passport has been increased to Rs 7,500, while a 60-page replacement passport will cost Rs 8,500.2. ITR-1, ITR-2 deadlinesFor taxpayers filing ITR-1 and ITR-2 forms, the due date for filing returns for the Financial Year 2025-26 (Assessment Year 2026-27) is July 31, 2026. Missing the prescribed deadlines could result in penalties, restrictions on choosing certain tax regimes and limitations on carrying forward eligible losses to future assessment years.3. HDFC Credit Card ChangesFrom July 1, 2026, HDFC Bank credit card holders can enjoy up to three complimentary domestic airport lounge visits every calendar quarter. However, this benefit will be available only if they spend at least Rs 60,000 in the previous calendar quarter.For example, to qualify for complimentary lounge access during the July-September 2026 quarter, cardholders must spend a minimum of Rs 60,000 between April and June 2026. The same spend-based eligibility criteria will continue to apply for each subsequent calendar quarter.4. Free update to the Aadhaar cardFrom July 1, 2026, the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) will make it free to update the registered email address linked to your Aadhaar card. The usual Rs 75 update fee has been waived for a limited period, allowing users to avail of the service at no cost until December 31, 2026.According to an official notification, "It has been decided to waive the charges (i.e., Rs 75) for availing the service of email address update through the Aadhaar mobile application and make it free of cost for a period of six months with effect from July 1, 2026, to December 31, 2026."5. SBI credit card changesSBI Card has announced changes to the reward point programme for select PhonePe SBI Credit Cards. The change will come into effect from July 1, 2026. The revision affects both PhonePe SBI Credit Card PURPLE and PhonePe SBI Credit Card SELECT BLACK cardholders, as new limits on earning reward points and a broader list of transactions that won't earn reward points have been introduced.6. HDFC Credit Card ChangesEffective July 1, 2026, HDFC Bank credit card holders can avail of up to three complimentary domestic airport lounge visits every calendar quarter. To qualify for this benefit, cardholders must spend at least Rs 60,000 in the immediately preceding calendar quarter.Also read: EPFO 3.0 Update: UPI and ATM PF withdrawal facility launch date, limit, and new rules decoded

title: Delhi EV Policy Approved - Check How You Can Benefit And Save Big
description: Delhi Cabinet has approved the EV Policy 2026, finalising subsidies, tax waivers and timelines for phasing out petrol vehicles. This policy benefits two-wheelers, autos, and cars with significant incentives for early adopters.
content: Delhi Cabinet has finally cleared the EV Policy 2026 and the subsidies, tax waivers as well as petrol-vehicle phase-out dates are now locked in rather than sitting around as draft proposals. If you're in Delhi and have been putting off an EV purchase waiting for "official" numbers, this is that moment, the policy covers two-wheelers, autos, and cars, and the figures are worth running through before you head to a dealership. Here's a detailed look at what changes will the new EV policy will bring about.  What Do You Get in Subsidies?Start with two-wheelers. If the bike or scooter you're eyeing is priced up to Rs 2.25 lakh ex-factory, you're looking at Rs 10,000 per kWh, capped at Rs 30,000, but only in year one. That drops to Rs 20,000 in year two and just Rs 10,000 by year three, so timing genuinely matters here. Three-wheeler buyers get a similar sliding scale: Rs 50,000, then Rs 40,000, then Rs 30,000 across the same three years. Cars get a different kind of break, a full waiver on road tax and registration fees for any EV priced up to Rs 30 lakh with hybrids getting half that waiver and this runs until March 2030. Now, about that Rs 1 lakh headline number for cars, it's not a blanket bonus everyone gets. It's tied to scrapping: junk a BS-IV or older vehicle and replace it with an EV under Rs 30 lakh within six months, and that's when the extra Rs 1 lakh kicks in, stacked on top of the tax waiver.When Do Petrol Autos and Two-Wheelers Go Away?These aren't soft targets, they're real cutoff dates. From January 1, 2027, you simply won't be able to register a new petrol or CNG auto in Delhi, only electric ones. Two-wheelers get a bit more runway with the same rule landing on April 1, 2028. That date matters more than people realise, since two-wheelers account for roughly two-thirds of every vehicle on Delhi's roads.Delivery and ride-hailing platforms, think Swiggy, Zomato, Ola, Uber, are being squeezed earlier still: no new petrol or diesel two-wheelers or light goods vehicles in their fleets from January 2026, and any BS6 vehicles already in service have until the end of 2026 to switch over.Is There a Catch Here?Mostly just one: every incentive in this policy shrinks the longer you wait. Buy now and you're at the top of the ladder; wait two or three years and you're getting a fraction of what early movers got. The government's put nearly Rs 4,000 crore behind all this, split between purchase incentives, scrapping bonuses, and charging infrastructure, including a dedicated Rs 1,000 crore push to grow Delhi's charging points from around 9,000 today to 36,000 by 2030. If an EV was already on your radar, there's a genuine money-on-the-table reason not to sit on it too long.

title: Compelled to fast again, may even die but won't step back: Sonam Wangchuk on Ladakh protest
description:
New Delhi, Jun 29: Climate activist and educator Sonam Wangchuk, who began an indefinite fast at the ongoing CJP protest at Jantar Mantar here on Monday, said he had hoped he would never have to return to agitation, but was left with no choice as promises made to the people of Ladakh remained unfulfilled and dialogue with the Centre had stalled.
In an interview with PTI, Wangchuk, who has been at the forefront of Ladakh's movement for constitutional safeguards, including Sixth Schedule status, said he remained committed to dialogue and hoped his hunger strike would revive meaningful engagement.
"I was thinking I would not be required to sit on a protest. I am sad I have to sit on a fast again. I am not doing it happily; it is not easy either. I may even die, but even if I die, I will not step back," Wangchuk told PTI.
Wangchuk's latest agitation comes amid the prolonged movement in Ladakh over demands for constitutional safeguards, statehood and greater political representation for the Union Territory.
The demand for bringing Ladakh under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution has remained one of the key issues raised by civil society groups from the Union Territory
Recalling the dialogue process with the Centre, Wangchuk, who was detained under the National Security Act (NSA) in September 2025 during the Ladakh protests and released in February 2026, expressed hope that meetings held during and after his detention would help rebuild trust.
"February 4, when I was in jail, one meeting was held, which was nearly unsuccessful. May 22, after my release, another meeting was held. I had a lot of hope with that because it was said they released me to create an atmosphere of trust and that the dialogue would continue," he said.
According to Wangchuk, the May 22 meeting had offered an opportunity to move forward, but he alleged that the conclusions were not formally recorded.
"The May 22 meeting was like that, but they are now shying away from putting the conclusions on paper. This was an opportunity to build trust with the people. Taking one step forward and two steps backwards, people lost faith," he said.
Wangchuk said the trust deficit was rooted in earlier assurances as well.
"There was already a trust deficit because after making promises in 2013-14, they went back on their words," he said, referring to the demand for Sixth Schedule safeguards.
However, he insisted that he had not lost faith in dialogue.
"I still have hope, else I would have also committed suicide, but I am still here. It means I still have hope. We hope their conscience will awaken and they will realise they are doing wrong. They had promised it in writing," he said.
Wangchuk said governments should respond to people's concerns with empathy and avoid treating dissent as a threat.
"If you consider India a democracy... if you don't, if you think of it as a totalitarian authoritarian government, then you can have a stern approach, but then stop calling yourself a democracy," he said.
Calling for accountability, Wangchuk said the issues raised by the protesters should be debated in Parliament.
"There should be accountability. One resignation may not change everything, but we would want this to be raised in the upcoming session. There should be a debate," he said.
Appealing to political parties to support the movement, Wangchuk said they should look beyond politics and think of future generations.
"I urge all parties, you are a part of this movement if you stand for the generations to come. Adopt a positive approach. Consider this a blessing that people are still raising their voices. If they were dead, they would not raise their voice. You don't want a graveyard," he said.
He also urged protesters to remain peaceful.
"Don't have fear, but also don't have hatred. Raise your message with flowers. All people of India would be with you. When people are with you, no government would be able to ignore your demands," he said.
Drawing from his own experience of detention, Wangchuk encouraged young protesters not to fear imprisonment.
"Don't be scared of jail, I can tell by my personal experience, it will transform you. Don't fear anything. You will get justice," he said.
Reiterating that his movement was centred on education and environmental protection, Wangchuk said Ladakh's concerns should not be allowed to linger.
"My two eyes are education and environment. Regarding the environment, I am sitting here to raise the issue of Ladakh and call for it to be resolved.
"Don't let it become a festering wound. It is a border area which is very sensitive. Keeping it hanging is not in favour of the country," he said.
